author: @williamspaid8829
text: Please correct if I'm wrong,  but I thought electric motors made more heat when they were under powered?

replies.author: @Nick_Tag
replies.text: This is basically saying there is an efficiency drop and the size in that drop is greater than the total rate of waste heat energy at higher voltages of operation. I’m no expert on universal motors but would assume once you’ve put it enough energy for the motor to overcome it’s own inertia the build up of heat (motor’s temperature) is going to be more a function of the length of time it is operated for, *after* which it becomes ‘under powered’ due to the now different thermal properties / operating regime.

author: @nil5221
text: I know its a bit late in the process but have you considered simply starting the generator on petrol to draw the air through while firing up the gasifier, then turning off the petrol & running on syngas. That would also overcome the problem of choke/fuel enrichment while starting a cold engine, it would be already warmed up. No air pump, no electricity/battery needed...just keeps it simpler. Apologies if this has been mentioned before.

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: Also, that would mean that you could hook it up and try it out RIGHT NOW! well OK then, I'm in Oz, so after you get some sleep :-) We're all impatient to see it work lol

replies.author: @another3997
replies.text: Thank you for your thoughts. There are some products which are getting low, but the supermarket shelves seem to be well stocked with most things, for the time being at least. There's no desperate shortages, so as long as people are sensible (yeah, it's a big ask) things should be fine. Hopefully, once the shipping ports start moving again, everything will be back to 'normal' fairly soon. Normal for 2020. 😁

replies.author: @totherarf

author: @MrJamespcastle
text: I have built  several gasifiers. The uncooled flare tube gets ridiculously hot.

author: @BradKarthauser
text: Interestingly, I was watching another channel and the person turned off the blower once the generator started. The generator then self regulated it's own gas intake.  If you've already planned that or mentioned it in a video - I apologize.  I'm on a short break and wanted to dash off a note.  Cheers!

author: @MrJamespcastle
text:
I am concerned about the use as a gasifier blower. The astic may get to hot and melt. Even if it doesnt melt completely, it is most likely going to warp the impeller until it isn't effective anymore.
I suggest you use the plastic parts as a mold and cast the parts out of aluminum. That would make a great video!

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: Except that this blower (more like a sucker?) is the very last component of the system, where the generator engine carburettor is. The gas is cool at this stage.
